title: turnera sidoides subsp. pinnatifida
intro: Turnera sidoides subsp. pinnatifida, also known as Mayan gold or turnera, is a plant species in the family Passifloraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. Mayan gold can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall well-being.
